question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: touchMarket / setDefaultSettlementFee] Can an unprivileged market creator, by calling touchMarket on a market whose loanToken has a pending feeSetter-queued defaultSettlementFeeCbp update, front-run the feeSetter's setDefaultSettlementFee transaction to snapshot the old (lower) default fee into the new market's settlementFeeCbp0..6, causing all subsequent takes in that market to pay a lower settlement fee than the feeSetter intended, violating the invariant that market fees must reflect the current protocol fee policy at creation time, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between default fees and market fees? Proof idea: fuzz (loanToken, defaultFee_before, defaultFee_after, market_creation_timestamp) and assert marketState[id].settlementFeeCbp* == defaultSettlementFeeCbp[loanToken][*] at creation time, then assert they diverge after setDefaultSettlementFee is called post-creation.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / touchMarket] Can an attacker acting as a taker call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral on a not-yet-created market, causing the bundler to call touchMarket and snapshot the current defaultSettlementFeeCbp into the new market, while a concurrent feeSetter transaction to raise defaultSettlementFeeCbp is pending in the mempool, resulting in the market being permanently created with the lower fee, violating the invariant that market creation must use the fee in effect at the time of the first touch, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between default fees and market fees? Proof idea: simulate two transactions in the same block with different orderings and assert the resulting marketState[id].settlementFeeCbp6 differs.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: claimSettlementFee] Can the feeClaimer call claimSettlementFee(token, amount, receiver) for a token whose corresponding market has never been created (tickSpacing == 0), draining claimableSettlementFee[token] that was accumulated from a different market sharing the same loanToken, violating the invariant that fee claims must correspond to valid market accounting, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between created and uncreated markets? Proof idea: create market A with loanToken T, accumulate fees via take, then call claimSettlementFee(T, amount, attacker) without specifying market A, and assert claimableSettlementFee[T] decreases without any market-existence check.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: setDefaultSettlementFee / touchMarket] Can the feeSetter call setDefaultSettlementFee for a loanToken to set all 7 breakpoints to 0, then an attacker creates a new market for that loanToken via touchMarket, permanently locking that market's settlement fees at 0 for all TTM breakpoints, meaning all takes in that market pay zero settlement fee and claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] never increases, violating the invariant that settlement fees must be claimable by the feeClaimer, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between default fees and market fees? Proof idea: set defaultSettlementFeeCbp[token][*] = 0, create market, take, assert claimableSettlementFee[token] == 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: setDefaultContinuousFee / touchMarket] Can the feeSetter set defaultContinuousFee[loanToken] to MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE, then an attacker creates a market via touchMarket, causing the market's continuousFee to be set to MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE at creation, while all previously created markets for the same loanToken retain their original continuousFee, creating a permanent fee inconsistency between markets of the same loanToken, violating the invariant that fee parameters must be consistently applied across markets, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between default fees and market fees? Proof idea: fuzz (loanToken, fee_before, fee_after, market_creation_order) and assert marketState[id1].continuousFee != marketState[id2].continuousFee for same loanToken.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: setMarketContinuousFee] Can the feeSetter call setMarketContinuousFee(id, 0) on an existing market to zero out its continuousFee, while defaultContinuousFee[loanToken] remains non-zero, causing new markets created after this point to have a higher continuousFee than the modified market, creating an inconsistency where lenders in the modified market accrue zero continuous fee while lenders in new markets accrue the default fee, violating the invariant that fee changes must be consistently applied, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between default fees and market fees? Proof idea: assert marketState[id_old].continuousFee == 0 while defaultContinuousFee[loanToken] > 0 and marketState[id_new].continuousFee > 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: setFeeClaimer / claimContinuousFee] Can the roleSetter call setFeeClaimer(newClaimer) while the old feeClaimer has unclaimed continuousFeeCredit accumulated in multiple markets, causing the old claimer to permanently lose access to those fees (since claimContinuousFee requires msg.sender == feeClaimer), while the new claimer gains access to all accumulated fees, violating the invariant that fee claims must be attributable to the correct claimer, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency in role-controlled parameters? Proof idea: accumulate continuousFeeCredit in market, change feeClaimer, assert old claimer's claimContinuousFee reverts with OnlyFeeClaimer().

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: setRoleSetter] Can the current roleSetter call setRoleSetter(address(0)), permanently bricking all future role updates (setFeeSetter, setFeeClaimer, setTickSpacingSetter, setRoleSetter), while existing role holders retain their current roles indefinitely, violating the invariant that role-controlled parameters must remain governable, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency in role-controlled parameters? Proof idea: call setRoleSetter(address(0)), then attempt setFeeSetter(x) and assert it reverts with OnlyRoleSetter().

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: settlementFee / touchMarket] Can any caller invoke settlementFee(id, timeToMaturity) for a market id where marketState[id].tickSpacing == 0 (uncreated market), causing a revert with MarketNotCreated(), while the same id's defaultSettlementFeeCbp values are non-zero in the default mapping, creating a state where the fee is queryable via defaultSettlementFeeCbp but not via settlementFee, violating the invariant that fee state must be consistently readable before and after market creation, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between created and uncreated markets? Proof idea: assert settlementFee(id, ttm) reverts for uncreated id while defaultSettlementFeeCbp[loanToken][i] > 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: touchMarket / setDefaultSettlementFee] Can the feeSetter call setDefaultSettlementFee for a loanToken after a market for that token is already created, causing the market's per-market settlementFeeCbp* to remain at the old default while the global default changes, and then a second market for the same loanToken is created with the new default, resulting in two live markets with different settlement fees for the same loanToken and same TTM, violating the invariant that markets with identical parameters should have identical fee schedules, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between default fees and market fees? Proof idea: create market1, change default, create market2, assert settlementFee(id1, ttm) != settlementFee(id2, ttm) for same ttm.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IPermit2.permitTransferFrom] Can an attacker replay a Permit2 signature (nonce, deadline, token, amount) that was originally signed for a MidnightBundles buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral call, by submitting the same signature in a second call before the deadline expires, causing a double-pull of loanToken from the victim's wallet, violating the invariant that Permit2 nonces must be consumed exactly once, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency in role-controlled parameters (authorization state)? Proof idea: sign Permit2 permit, call bundler once, call bundler again with same permit data, assert second call reverts at Permit2 level due to nonce consumption.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IPermit2.permitTransferFrom] Can an attacker who observes a victim's signed Permit2 PermitTransferFrom (nonce, deadline, token, amount) front-run the victim's bundler call by submitting the same signature directly to Permit2.permitTransferFrom with a different SignatureTransferDetails.to address, draining the victim's tokens to the attacker's address before the bundler call executes, violating the invariant that Permit2 signatures must only be consumable by the intended recipient, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between created and uncreated markets (token balance desync)? Proof idea: observe mempool signature, front-run with different 'to', assert victim's bundler call fails with Permit2 nonce already consumed.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / touchMarket] Can an attacker call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with a market that has never been touched, causing the bundler to call touchMarket and create the market with current default fees, then immediately take offers in the same transaction, while the market creator (attacker) benefits from a fee schedule that differs from what the feeSetter intended for that market, violating the invariant that market creation and fee assignment must be atomic with feeSetter intent, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between default fees and market fees? Proof idea: set defaultSettlementFeeCbp to attacker-favorable values, call bundler to create+take in one tx, assert fees differ from feeSetter's intended schedule.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee] Can the feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, newFee) for an existing market to set a specific breakpoint fee higher than the current default for that loanToken, while other markets for the same loanToken retain the lower default fee, creating a state where two markets with the same loanToken have different settlement fee schedules at the same TTM breakpoint, violating the invariant that settlement fees must be consistently applied across markets of the same loanToken, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between default fees and market fees? Proof idea: assert settlementFee(id1, 30 days) != settlementFee(id2, 30 days) for same loanToken after per-market override.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: continuousFee (view) / touchMarket] Can any caller read continuousFee(id) for an uncreated market (tickSpacing == 0), receiving 0 as the return value (since MarketState is zero-initialized), while defaultContinuousFee[loanToken] is non-zero, causing off-chain systems or integrations to incorrectly assume the market has zero continuous fee before creation, violating the invariant that fee state must accurately reflect the fee that will be applied upon market creation, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between created and uncreated markets? Proof idea: assert continuousFee(id) == 0 for uncreated id while defaultContinuousFee[loanToken] == MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: settlementFeeCbps (view) / touchMarket] Can any caller read settlementFeeCbps(id) for an uncreated market, receiving all-zeros array, while defaultSettlementFeeCbp[loanToken] has non-zero values, causing integrations to compute zero settlement fee for a market that will have non-zero fees upon creation, violating the invariant that pre-creation fee queries must not mislead about post-creation fee state, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between created and uncreated markets? Proof idea: assert settlementFeeCbps(id)[6] == 0 for uncreated id while defaultSettlementFeeCbp[loanToken][6] > 0, then create market and assert settlementFeeCbps(id)[6] > 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing / touchMarket] Can the tickSpacingSetter call setMarketTickSpacing(id, newSpacing) for a market that was just created with DEFAULT_TICK_SPACING = 4, reducing it to 1, while a second market with identical parameters but different maturity retains tickSpacing = 4, creating a state where two markets with the same loanToken and collateral have different tick spacings, violating the invariant that tick spacing must be consistently applied across markets unless explicitly overridden, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency in role-controlled parameters? Proof idea: create two markets, reduce spacing on one, assert marketState[id1].tickSpacing != marketState[id2].tickSpacing.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing] Can the tickSpacingSetter call setMarketTickSpacing(id, newSpacing) where newSpacing does not divide DEFAULT_TICK_SPACING (e.g., newSpacing = 3 when current is 4), causing the require(marketState[id].tickSpacing % newTickSpacing == 0) check to revert, but if the current spacing was already reduced to 6 and newSpacing = 3, the call succeeds, creating a state where tick spacing can be reduced to values that were not reachable from DEFAULT_TICK_SPACING = 4, violating the invariant that tick spacing reductions must only unlock ticks that are multiples of the original spacing, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency in role-controlled parameters? Proof idea: fuzz (initial_spacing, intermediate_spacing, final_spacing) and assert final_spacing is always a divisor of DEFAULT_TICK_SPACING.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: claimContinuousFee / setFeeClaimer] Can the feeClaimer call claimContinuousFee(market, amount, receiver) where amount > marketState[id].continuousFeeCredit, causing an underflow revert in _marketState.continuousFeeCredit -= toUint128(amount), while a concurrent setFeeClaimer call changes the feeClaimer, creating a race condition where neither the old nor new claimer can claim the accumulated fees, violating the invariant that accumulated fees must always be claimable, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency in role-controlled parameters? Proof idea: accumulate continuousFeeCredit, attempt overclaim, change feeClaimer, assert fees are stuck.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: touchMarket / setDefaultContinuousFee] Can an attacker create a market for a loanToken where defaultContinuousFee[loanToken] == 0 (never set by feeSetter), causing the market's continuousFee to be permanently 0 (since setMarketContinuousFee can only be called after creation and requires feeSetter), while the feeSetter later sets defaultContinuousFee[loanToken] > 0 for future markets, creating a permanent inconsistency where the attacker's market has zero continuous fee, violating the invariant that all markets must be subject to the protocol's fee policy, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between default fees and market fees? Proof idea: create market before feeSetter sets default, assert marketState[id].continuousFee == 0 while defaultContinuousFee[loanToken] > 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral / touchMarket] Can an attacker call repayAndWithdrawCollateral on an uncreated market, causing touchMarket to create the market with current default fees, then repay 0 units (since units = assets - referralFeeAssets and referralFeePct can be set to make units = 0), creating a market with no actual activity but with the current default fee snapshot, violating the invariant that market creation must be accompanied by meaningful state initialization, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between created and uncreated markets? Proof idea: call repayAndWithdrawCollateral with assets=1, referralFeePct=WAD-1, assert market is created with tickSpacing > 0 but no debt/credit.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: setFeeSetter / setDefaultSettlementFee] Can the roleSetter call setFeeSetter(address(0)), permanently preventing any future calls to setDefaultSettlementFee, setMarketSettlementFee, setDefaultContinuousFee, and setMarketContinuousFee (all require msg.sender == feeSetter), while existing market fees remain frozen at their current values, violating the invariant that fee parameters must remain adjustable by the protocol, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency in role-controlled parameters? Proof idea: setFeeSetter(address(0)), attempt setDefaultSettlementFee, assert revert with OnlyFeeSetter().

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: touchMarket / setDefaultSettlementFee] Can the feeSetter set defaultSettlementFeeCbp[loanToken][0] (0-day breakpoint) to MAX_SETTLEMENT_FEE_0_DAYS = 0.000014e18, then an attacker creates a market and immediately takes an offer at TTM = 0 (post-maturity), causing the settlement fee to equal settlementFeeCbp0 * CBP = 0.000014e18, which is the maximum allowed, while a market created before the default change has settlementFeeCbp0 = 0, creating a state where post-maturity takes have different costs in different markets, violating the invariant that post-maturity settlement fees must be consistent, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between default fees and market fees? Proof idea: create market1 (fee0=0), change default, create market2 (fee0=max), warp past maturity, assert settlementFee(id1, 0) != settlementFee(id2, 0).

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IPermit2.permitTransferFrom] Can an attacker craft a Permit2 PermitTransferFrom with token = loanToken, amount = maxBuyerAssets, nonce = N, deadline = far_future, and submit it to MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral, where the bundler calls IPermit2(PERMIT2).permitTransferFrom transferring tokens to address(this) (the bundler), but the bundler's forceApproveMax then approves MIDNIGHT for type(uint256).max, allowing a subsequent reentrancy or multicall to drain the bundler's token balance, violating the invariant that token pulls must be immediately consumed and not left in the bundler, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between created and uncreated markets? Proof idea: check bundler token balance after failed take loop, assert no residual balance remains.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium State inconsistency between created and uncreated markets, default fees and market fees, or role-controlled parameters'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee / setDefaultSettlementFee] Can the feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, 6, 0) to zero out the 360-day breakpoint fee for an existing market, while setDefaultSettlementFee(loanToken, 6, MAX_SETTLEMENT_FEE_360_DAYS) sets the default to maximum for new markets, creating a state where the existing market has zero fee at TTM >= 360 days while new markets have maximum fee, allowing takers in the existing market to take offers at TTM >= 360 days with zero settlement fee, violating the invariant that settlement fees must be non-zero when the default is non-zero, causing scoped impact: state inconsistency between default fees and market fees? Proof idea: assert settlementFee(id_old, 400 days) == 0 while settlementFee(id_new, 400 days) == MAX_SETTLEMENT_FEE_360_DAYS.
